name: Salmon"in My Style
recipeInstructions:
See Fish Fume and Crayfish Tail Salad recipes.
White Wine Sauce:
1. In a sauté pan, heat the butter over medium heat.
2. Add the shallots and mushrooms to the pan. Cook for about two minutes until they begin to soften.
3. Stir in the white wine and 1 cup of fish fume. Cook for an additional 5 minutes.
4. Add the heavy cream and bring to a simmer, stirring occasionally. Reduce the sauce by half (should be more white than gray) over low heat for about 20 minutes until it thickens enough to coat a spoon.
5. Season with salt and pepper before serving.
Fish:
1. Slice the salmon filets from the boned fish at an angle, about 1 to 1 1/2 inches thick.
2. Pound each fillet under dampened plastic wrap and parchment paper until evenly thin.
3. Season both sides of the salmon lightly with salt.
4. Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a non-stick pan over medium heat until it begins to shimmer but does not brown.
5. Add the salmon to the pan, being careful not to overcrowd the pan. Cook for about three to four minutes on each side.
6. Using tongs, remove the fillets from the pan and flip them once more before placing back in the pan to cook for another three to four minutes.
7. Remove the pan from heat and carefully turn the salmon one last time to allow the remaining juices to settle.
8. To serve, spoon sauce into a pool on a serving plate, place salad in the center of the plate, and arrange the salmon over the top.
9. Garnish with red basil leaves.
Note: The original recipe was written by Udo Nechutnys from The Miramonte Restaurant, San Francisco, CA, as published in Great Chefs of San Francisco (1984).
